key_insight: AA-ViT uses anatomy-, structure-, and frequency-guided vision transformer design to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI from non-contrast scans while better preserving lesion and boundary detail.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this could improve tumour visualization without gadolinium, potentially reducing scan burden and supporting patients who cannot safely receive contrast agents.
method: Takes T1, T2, and FLAIR as input for post-contrast MRI synthesis
Adds anatomical awareness plus structural and frequency guidance to improve boundary preservation
Compares against state-of-the-art synthesis methods on BraTS 2021
evidence_signal: On BraTS 2021, the model reported higher PSNR and SSIM than competing approaches, and three neuroradiologists plus one neurosurgeon gave 19 randomly selected cases a mean score of 3.94/5.
watch_item: Clinical utility is still preliminary, with limited reader evaluation and the need for broader multi-site validation before real-world deployment.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
